_comment: Epic Q baselines (ADR 0030). STRUCTURAL values gate HARD (exact, or summaryChars ± tolerancePct): a red gate is a real product change — update the baseline consciously, with reasoning in the commit, never by auto-regeneration. softCeilingsMs are ~10×-class sanity ceilings for wall-clock (generous on purpose; csharp/mixed cover a cold sidecar build incl. NuGet restore). csharp-small and mixed encode the Roslyn tier and are only gated where the dotnet CLI exists.

coldStartSla._comment: ADR 0034 S4 — conversational COLD-START latency SLAs, gated by test/latencySla.test.ts. Each target is built in a FRESH node process (true cold start: WASM tree-sitter grammars + the TS Program load from scratch — the two cold-start drivers ADR 0034 names). Unlike the per-fixture softCeilingsMs (a ~10× wall-clock SANITY ceiling on a within-process init), these are real product budgets: the first deep tool call must return under slaMs even on a slow CI runner. Honoring ADR 0030's anti-flakiness mandate, slaMs keeps generous headroom over devBaselineMs (machine-relative; a slow runner needing a bump is a conscious edit here, with reasoning — never auto-regenerated). FOUR complementary targets cover distinct cold-start drivers/failure modes: 'cpp-namespaces' = the WASM tree-sitter grammar-load path; 'csharp-small' = the ROSLYN sidecar warm-up (process spawn + JIT + first compilation — the heaviest driver ADR 0034 names alongside the TS Program; dotnet-gated, measured with the sidecar PRE-BUILT so it's the stable per-session cold start, NOT the one-time NuGet build); 'self' = this repository (a realistic ~144-file multi-language medium repo: TS source + cpp/python/… fixtures → WASM), gitignore-scoped, the hardest real-world COMPLEXITY cold-start (coldMs grows with the repo, so a breach is a genuine regression signal); 'large' = a GENERATED ~5000-file synthetic TS repo (temp dir, cleaned up; ~10k nodes) gating cold-start SCALING in file COUNT at genuinely-large scale — it catches an O(n²)-class regression (and scaling cliffs that only appear past ~1000 files) in the walk/graph-build/index. NOTE: file count is NOT the dominant cold-start cost — 5000 trivial synthetic files (~2.2s, ~0.44ms/file) is comparable in WALL-CLOCK to just 144 real multi-language files (~2.8s, ~19ms/file): real per-file cost is ~44× higher (deep dep graphs, @types, real inference, symbol density). So 'large' is a count-SCALING guard, not a complexity test, and a faithful large-real-COMPLEXITY SLA still needs a representative real repo (a separate experiment confirmed even an elaborately type-complex synthetic — generics/interfaces/cross-file types — measured ~27× too cheap per file to substitute).
